data.title: Bitmine increased its ETH holdings by 71,524 last week, bringing its total staked ETH to over 3.33 million.
data.description: Ethereum treasury firm Bitmine Immersion Technologies disclosed that it increased its holdings of 71,524 ETH last week. Its current cryptocurrency holdings include 4,874,858 ETH, 198 BTC, $85 million worth of Eightco Holdings equity, and $200 million worth of Beast Industries shares. In addition, the company has a total of 3,334,637 ETH staked (equivalent to $7.4 billion at $2,206 per ETH). (PRNewswire)

data.title: OpenAI praised its alliance with Amazon in a memo.
data.description: In a memo, OpenAI praised its alliance with Amazon, stating that Microsoft "limited its ability to reach customers." (CNBC)

data.title: Empery Digital sold another 55 bitcoins, reducing its BTC holdings to 2,934.
data.description: Nasdaq-listed Bitcoin treasury company Empery Digital disclosed that it sold 55 bitcoins last week at an average price of $70,560, generating approximately $3.9 million in revenue. Its total Bitcoin holdings have decreased to 2,934 bitcoins. The company also disclosed that it has spent approximately $147 million to repurchase 25,805,396 shares of common stock and will continue to sell Bitcoin as needed to fund future share buybacks and potentially repay some outstanding debt. (Businesswire)

data.title: Binance to Launch TreehouseFi Booster Event with $350,000 TREE Token Rewards
data.description:
Binance announced on X the upcoming return of the TreehouseFi Booster Event, offering participants a chance to share in $350,000 worth of TREE tokens. The sixth edition of this event is scheduled to run from April 14, 2026, at 8:00 to June 13, 2026, at 7:59, East Eight Zone Time. Participants can earn rewards by staking wstETH in the Treehouse wstETH fund pool through Binance Wallet Finance.

The event specifies that only subscription shares acquired during the event period will be eligible for reward calculations. Holdings in the relevant fund pool prior to the start of the event will not be considered for rewards. This initiative provides an opportunity for users to engage with the platform and potentially benefit from the TREE token distribution. Participants are encouraged to prepare for the event and ensure their subscriptions are made within the designated timeframe to qualify for the rewards.

data.title: Finance Leaders Confident in Gulf's Resilience Amid Iran Conflict
data.description: Finance leaders are expressing confidence in the Gulf region's ability to withstand the economic impacts of the ongoing conflict involving Iran. Bloomberg posted on X that despite the geopolitical tensions, major financial institutions are betting on the Gulf's economic stability and resilience. The region's strategic importance and robust economic fundamentals are seen as key factors that could mitigate potential fallout from the conflict. Analysts highlight the Gulf's diversified economy and strong fiscal policies as reasons for optimism. The situation continues to be monitored closely by investors and policymakers alike.

data.title: Polymarket's probability that "the Strait of Hormuz will return to normal by May 31" is only 37%, and the market has fallen 9% in the last 24 hours.
data.description: According to Odaily Seer's monitoring, Polymarket's probability of "the Strait of Hormuz returning to normal by May 31st" is only 37%, a 9% drop in 24 hours. The contract rules for this event state that if the International Monetary Fund's Portwatch reports a 7-day moving average of vessel arrivals ("vessel arrivals") in the Strait of Hormuz equal to or greater than 60 on any date between the market's creation and May 31, 2026, the market will be rated "Yes"; otherwise, it will be rated "No". Daily vessel arrivals include container ships, bulk carriers, roll-on/roll-off ships, general cargo ships, and tankers; vessels not reported by IMF Portwatch will not be considered. According to Xinhua News Agency, the US military announced it will begin implementing a blockade of the Gulf of Oman and the Arabian Sea east of the Strait of Hormuz, and any unauthorized entry into or departure from the blockade area may be intercepted, diverted, and detained. In addition, Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu expressed support for Trump's decision to impose a naval blockade on Iran, adding that his government is maintaining full coordination with Washington on the matter. According to a video statement released by the Prime Minister's office, Netanyahu stated at a cabinet meeting: "Iran has violated the rules, and President Trump has decided to impose a naval blockade." "We certainly support this firm stance, and we are maintaining continuous coordination with the United States." Odaily Seer continues to focus on prediction markets, seeing changes before pricing them in.

data.title: India Faces Economic Challenges Amid Rising Prices and Gas Shortage
data.description: India is currently grappling with significant economic challenges, as reported by Bloomberg posted on X. The country is experiencing a double whammy of rising prices and a gas shortage, which is impacting various sectors. Menaka Doshi highlights the inflationary pressures that are affecting the economy, while Satviki Sanjay focuses on the difficulties faced by Firozabad's glass makers due to the gas shortage. These issues are contributing to a complex economic environment, requiring careful management and strategic solutions to mitigate their effects.

data.title: Brazil Economists Adjust 2026 Inflation Forecast Amid Energy Shock
data.description: Brazilian economists have increased their inflation forecast for 2026, surpassing the central bank's target ceiling. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the impact of the ongoing energy shock resulting from the Iran war. The adjustment reflects concerns over rising energy prices and their potential effects on the Brazilian economy. Economists are closely monitoring the situation as they anticipate further implications for inflation and economic stability in the region.

data.title: Iran has warned that if its ports are threatened, no port in the region is safe.
data.description: The Trump administration's move to exert maximum pressure on Iran by blocking the Strait of Hormuz has prompted a strong response from Iran, which has threatened retaliation against all ports in the Gulf. The crude oil market is already experiencing tight spot supply, with some refineries even paying record premiums of up to $50 per barrel. The U.S. Central Command stated that the blockade will begin at 10:00 AM Eastern Time on Monday and will be "fairly enforced against all vessels traveling to and from Iranian ports and coastal areas, covering all Iranian ports in the Persian Gulf and the Gulf of Oman." JPMorgan analysts stated in a report: "Reopening the Strait of Hormuz has become the most time-sensitive priority for the market. The last oil tanker to pass through the strait on February 28 is expected to arrive at its destination around April 20, at which point pre-war inventories will be completely depleted from the global supply chain." Trump has long claimed that rising U.S. gasoline prices are only temporary. However, he admitted on Fox News' "Sunday Briefing" that high oil prices could persist until the midterm elections in November. (Jinshi)

data.title: OPEC Maintains U.S. Economic Growth Forecasts for 2026 and 2027
data.description: OPEC has released its monthly report, maintaining its forecast for U.S. economic growth at 2.2% for 2026 and 2% for 2027. According to Jin10, these projections reflect OPEC's assessment of the U.S. economic outlook over the next two years. The organization has not adjusted its expectations from previous reports, indicating a stable view of the U.S. economy's future performance. This forecast comes amid ongoing discussions about global economic trends and potential impacts on energy markets.

data.title: Strategy's STRC Preferred Stock Dividend and Short Interest Insights
data.description: Strategy's STRC preferred stock offers an annualized dividend of 11.5% on $5.3 billion outstanding. According to NS3.AI, this structure could incentivize hedge funds to increase short positions on MSTR. Currently, short interest surpasses 35 million MSTR shares, accounting for 11% of the float.

data.title: Strategy disclosed that it added 13,927 bitcoins last week.
data.description: According to market sources, Strategy disclosed that it increased its holdings of Bitcoin by 13,927 coins last week, worth approximately $1 billion.

data.title: Michael Saylor: Strategy's Bitcoin return year-to-date is 5.6%.
data.description: Michael Saylor stated in an article on the X platform that after purchasing 13,927 BTC, Strategy's total Bitcoin holdings reached 780,897, with a total acquisition cost of approximately $59.02 billion and a Bitcoin return of 5.6% since the beginning of 2026.

data.title: Pakistan, Egypt, and Türkiye will continue dialogue with the United States and Iran.
data.description: According to AXIOS: Pakistan, Egypt, and Türkiye will continue dialogue with the United States and Iran. (Jinshi)

data.title: GFL to Acquire Secure Waste in C$6.4 Billion Deal
data.description: GFL Environmental Inc. has announced an agreement to acquire Secure Waste in a transaction valued at approximately C$6.4 billion ($4.6 billion). Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting that this acquisition aims to expand GFL's presence in western Canada and enhance its services in the industrial and energy-linked waste sectors. This strategic move is expected to bolster GFL's market position and operational capabilities in these regions.

data.title: ECB Vice President Guindos: Rate Hike Depends on Secondary Effects
data.description: European Central Bank Vice President Luis de Guindos stated that any potential interest rate hikes will be contingent upon secondary effects. According to Jin10, Guindos emphasized the importance of monitoring these effects closely to determine the appropriate monetary policy actions. The ECB continues to assess economic indicators and market conditions to guide its decisions on interest rates.

data.title: The U.S. military will impose a blockade on the Arabian Sea and the Gulf of Oman east of the Strait of Hormuz.
data.description: A notice to crew members indicates that the U.S. military stated any vessels entering or leaving the blockade zone without authorization will be intercepted, diverted, and detained. The blockade applies to all shipping traffic, regardless of flag. The blockade will be implemented in the Gulf of Oman and the Arabian Sea east of the Strait of Hormuz. (Jin Shi)

data.title: Atomicals Market, a third-party Bitcoin marketplace, plans to cease operations on April 30.
data.description: Atomicals Market, a third-party Bitcoin marketplace, announced on the X platform that it plans to officially cease operations on April 30. Currently, all order and bid data are recorded off-chain and will no longer be displayed after the shutdown. Users do not need to perform any additional operations on order placement or pricing, but they need to transfer assets or export their AM Wallet private keys before the shutdown.

data.title: Israeli Prime Minister: Supports Trump's naval blockade against Iran
data.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu stated that Israel supports US President Donald Trump's decision to impose a naval blockade on Iran, adding that his government is maintaining full coordination with Washington on this matter. According to a video statement released by the Prime Minister's Office, Netanyahu said at a cabinet meeting: "Iran has violated the rules, and President Trump has decided to impose a naval blockade." "We certainly support this firm position, and we are maintaining continuous coordination with the United States." (Jin Shi)

data.title: US military: Strait of Hormuz to be closed; unauthorized vessels will not be allowed to pass.
data.description: The U.S. military announced that it will impose a blockade on the Gulf of Oman and the Arabian Sea east of the Strait of Hormuz, and any vessels entering or leaving the blockade area without authorization may be intercepted, diverted, and detained. (Xinhua)
